title: Alternate Interior Angles Are Congruent
description: When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, alternate interior angles have equal measure.
condition: Two parallel lines cut by a transversal; a pair of alternate interior angles identified.
response: Set the measures of the alternate interior angles equal.
label: Principle
example: If $\angle 1$ and $\angle 2$ are alternate interior angles and $m\angle 1 = 45^\circ$, then $m\angle 2 = 45^\circ$.

title: Area of a Parallelogram Using Base and Height
description: Compute the area of a parallelogram by multiplying its base by its corresponding height.
condition: Given a parallelogram with a known base length and perpendicular height.
response: Calculate area as base × height.
label: Principle / Model
example: If a parallelogram has base $4$ units and height $3$ units, then $A = 4 \times 3 = 12$ square units.

title: Area of Parallelogram by Decomposition
description: Find the area of a parallelogram by decomposing it into rectangles and/or triangles and summing or subtracting areas.
condition: Given a parallelogram on a grid that can be decomposed into simpler shapes.
response: Add and/or subtract the areas of component shapes to find total area.
label: Principle / Model
example: A parallelogram can be seen as a $3\times3$ square plus two triangles that combine to form a $3\times1$ rectangle, so total area $= 9 + 3 = 12$.

title: Classify a Rigid Transformation
description: Identify whether a transformation between two figures is a translation, rotation, or reflection.
condition: Given a preimage and image on a grid.
response: State whether the transformation is a translation, rotation, or reflection.
label: Concept
example: If Figure B is a mirror image of Figure A across a line, classify the transformation as a reflection.

title: Congruence via Rigid Transformations
description: Recognize that two figures are congruent if one can be mapped onto the other using a sequence of rigid transformations.
condition: Given two polygons on a coordinate grid.
response: Determine whether a sequence of translations, rotations, and/or reflections maps one figure exactly onto the other.
label: Principle / Model
example: If Shape A can be translated and then rotated so that it coincides exactly with Shape B, then $A \cong B$.
notes: Based on the definition of congruence in terms of rigid transformations.

title: Corresponding Angles in Rigid Transformations
description: Identify that corresponding angles in a figure and its image under a rigid transformation have equal measure.
condition: A figure is mapped to an image by a rigid transformation and corresponding vertices are known.
response: State that corresponding angles (e.g., ∠A and ∠E) have equal measure.
label: Principle / Model
example: If triangle $ABC$ is rotated to triangle $EDC$, then $\angle A = \angle E$ and $\angle ABC = \angle EDC$ because rigid transformations preserve angle measure.

title: Corresponding Side Lengths in Rigid Transformations
description: Identify that corresponding side lengths in a figure and its image under a rigid transformation are equal.
condition: A figure is mapped to an image by a rigid transformation and corresponding vertices are known.
response: State that corresponding side lengths (e.g., AB and ED) are equal.
label: Principle / Model
example: If triangle $ABC$ is rotated to triangle $EDC$, then $AB = ED$, $BC = DC$, and $AC = EC$ because rigid transformations preserve distance.

title: Definition of Vertical Angles
description: Identify vertical angles as opposite angles formed by two intersecting lines that share a common vertex.
condition: Two lines intersect forming opposite angle pairs.
response: State that the opposite angles are vertical angles.
label: Concept
example: If lines intersect at point $M$, the opposite angles at $M$ are vertical angles.

title: Describe a 90° Counterclockwise Rotation
description: State a 90-degree counterclockwise rotation about a specified center as part of a transformation sequence.
condition: Given a figure and instruction to rotate 90° counterclockwise around a specified point.
response: State that each point is turned 90° counterclockwise about the given center (e.g., 'Rotate 90° counterclockwise about point D').
label: Rule / Plan
example: After translating so $A$ is at $D$, rotate triangle $ABC$ $90^\circ$ counterclockwise about point $D$ to align with $DEF$.
notes: Focus is on describing the action, not computing coordinates.

title: Linear Pair Angles Are Supplementary
description: Two adjacent angles forming a straight line sum to 180 degrees.
condition: Two adjacent angles form a straight line (linear pair).
response: Set their measures to sum to $180^\circ$ and solve for the unknown angle.
label: Principle
example: If $m\angle a = 45^\circ$ and $\angle a$ and $\angle b$ form a linear pair, then $m\angle b = 180^\circ - 45^\circ = 135^\circ$.

title: Parallelism After 180° Rotation
description: Recognize that a segment and its image under a 180° rotation are parallel.
condition: A line segment is rotated 180° about a point not on the segment.
response: Conclude that the image segment is parallel to the original segment.
label: Principle / Model
example: If segment $CD$ is rotated $180^\circ$ about point $E$ to form $HJ$, then $HJ \parallel CD$.

title: Read Coordinates from a Coordinate Grid
description: Identify the ordered pair representing a plotted point on a coordinate plane.
condition: Given a plotted point on a standard coordinate grid with labeled axes.
response: State its coordinates as (x, y) by reading horizontal position first, then vertical position.
label: Production / Skill
example: Point $B$ is located 3 units right and 2 units up, so its coordinates are $(3, 2)$.

title: Reflect a Point Across the x-Axis
description: Determine the image of a point under reflection across the x-axis by negating the y-coordinate.
condition: Given a point (x, y) and a reflection across the x-axis.
response: State the image as (x, -y).
label: Principle / Model
example: Reflect $A(-4, 5)$ across the x-axis: $(-4, -5)$.
notes: x-coordinate remains unchanged; y-coordinate changes sign.

title: Reflect Point Across the y-Axis
description: Determine the image of a point after reflection across the y-axis.
condition: Given a point $(x, y)$ and reflection across the y-axis.
response: Produce the point $(-x, y)$.
label: Principle / Model
example: Reflect $(1,-3)$ across the y-axis to get $(-1,-3)$.

title: Rotate a Point 180° About a Center
description: Construct the image of a point under a 180° rotation about a given center by placing it directly opposite the center at the same distance.
condition: Given a point P and a center O, rotate P by 180° about O.
response: Place the image point P' so that O is the midpoint of segment PP'.
label: Production / Skill
example: If $C$ is rotated $180^\circ$ about $E$, then $C'$ lies on line $CE$ with $E$ the midpoint of $CC'$.
notes: Often performed geometrically without verbal justification.

title: Rotate a Point 90° Clockwise About a Center
description: Construct the image of a point under a 90° clockwise rotation about a given center while preserving distance.
condition: Given a point P and a center O, rotate P 90° clockwise about O.
response: Place the image point P' so that OP' = OP and angle POP' is 90° clockwise.
label: Production / Skill
example: If $D$ is rotated $90^\circ$ clockwise about $M$, then $D'$ satisfies $MD' = MD$ and $\angle DMD' = 90^\circ$ clockwise.

title: Rotation Preserves Side Lengths and Angles
description: Recognize that a rotation produces a congruent figure with equal corresponding side lengths and angle measures.
condition: A figure is rotated rigidly about a point.
response: State that corresponding sides and angles remain equal in measure.
label: Principle / Model
example: After a $90^\circ$ rotation, a side of length $2.2$ units remains $2.2$ units, and a $72^\circ$ angle remains $72^\circ$.

title: Sequence Transformations to Map One Figure to Another
description: Describe an ordered sequence of transformations that maps a preimage figure onto a target image.
condition: Given two congruent figures in different positions/orientations on the plane.
response: State a correct ordered sequence of transformations (e.g., translate, then rotate) that maps the first figure to the second.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Translate triangle $ABC$ so that $A$ maps to $D$, then rotate $90^\circ$ counterclockwise about $D$ to obtain triangle $DEF$.

title: Straight Angle Measures 180 Degrees
description: An angle that forms a straight line has a measure of 180 degrees.
condition: An angle is identified as a straight angle.
response: State that its measure is $180^\circ$.
label: Fact
example: Angles forming a straight line at point $C$ have total measure $180^\circ$.

title: Translate a Point by Given Units
description: Apply a translation by adding or subtracting values to the x- and/or y-coordinates.
condition: Given a point $(x,y)$ and a translation such as 3 units left or 4 units up.
response: Adjust coordinates accordingly (e.g., left subtracts from x; up adds to y).
label: Principle / Model
example: Translate $( -1,-3 )$ left 3 units to get $( -4,-3 )$.

title: Triangle Angle Sum via Parallel Lines
description: The sum of the interior angles of a triangle is 180 degrees, justified using alternate interior angles and a straight line.
condition: A triangle is formed with one side extended and a line parallel to its base creating alternate interior angles.
response: Use angle congruence and a straight angle to conclude the triangle’s interior angles sum to $180^\circ$.
label: Principle
example: If $AB \parallel CD$, then $\angle CAB \cong \angle ECA$ and $\angle CBA \cong \angle BCD$. Since $\angle ECA + \angle ACB + \angle BCD = 180^\circ$, the angles of $\triangle ABC$ sum to $180^\circ$.

title: Two Reflections Over Same Line Return Original
description: Recognize that reflecting a figure over the same line twice results in the original figure.
condition: A figure is reflected over line $\ell$, then reflected again over the same line $\ell$.
response: Conclude the final image is the original figure.
label: Principle / Model
example: Reflect over line $\ell$, then reflect again over $\ell$; the composition is the identity transformation.

title: Vertical Angles Are Congruent
description: When two lines intersect, opposite (vertical) angles have equal measure.
condition: Two lines intersect forming vertical (opposite) angles.
response: Set the measures of the vertical angles equal.
label: Principle
example: If two lines intersect and $m\angle 1 = 45^\circ$, then the vertical angle also measures $45^\circ$.
